2011-04-01.title: Writer Resurfaces After Disappearing in China
2011-04-01.abstract: Yang Hengjun, a spy novelist and political blogger who lives in Sydney, disappeared on Sunday.

2011-04-01.title: Liberia Uneasily Linked to Ivory Coast Conflict
2011-04-01.abstract: At least 100,000 refugees have fled to Liberia, while Liberian mercenaries are heading in the opposite direction, to take part in the Ivorian conflict.

2011-04-01.title: Syria Offers Changes Before Renewed Protests
2011-04-01.abstract: Analysts said they were doubtful that the changes, coming a day after President Bashar al-Assad pointedly refused to make concessions, would amount to more than window dressing.

2011-04-01.title: Cleanup Questions as Radiation Spreads
2011-04-01.abstract: As it struggles with a nuclear crisis, Japan’s government must decide whether and how to clean up areas that have been heavily contaminated by radioactivity.

2011-04-01.title: Losses in Pakistani Haven Strain Afghan Taliban
2011-04-01.abstract: Killings, arrests and internal disputes have reached the Afghan Taliban even in their haven in Pakistan, security officials say.

2011-04-02.title: Protesters Scold Egypt’s Military Council
2011-04-02.abstract: Disenchantment with the military was the focus of many speeches and chants in Tahrir Square on Friday, where protesters lamented the lingering aspects of the old regime.

2011-04-02.title: Rebel Attack on Brega Ends in Stalemate
2011-04-02.abstract: Libyan rebels resumed their offensive on Friday by massing heavier weapons in an attack on this eastern oil town, but were repulsed in the fading light of afternoon and backtracked along a main road under fire.

2011-04-02.title: Religious Radicals’ Turn to Democracy Alarms Egypt
2011-04-02.abstract: Islamic fundamentalists are embracing democracy, alarming those who believe that they are seeking to put in place strict Islamic law through ballots.

2011-04-02.title: A New Tune for Egypt's Radical Muslims
2011-04-02.abstract: Egypt's fundamentalist Muslims are preaching at universities, advertising on streets, and politicizing in five-star hotels. Their goal: to influence the Muslim content in Egypt's new constitution.

2011-04-02.title: Salafi Movement Seeks a New Role in a New Egypt
2011-04-02.abstract: Salafism, a radical Islamic movement in Egypt, attempts to gain a foothold in the burgeoning democracy that has emerged in the wake of former President Hosni Mubarak’s abdication.

2011-04-03.title: Head of U.N. Panel Regrets Saying Israel Intentionally Killed Gazans
2011-04-03.abstract: Richard Goldstone retracted the central finding of a report examining Israel’s invasion of Gaza in 2008-9.

2011-04-03.title: Efforts to Plug Japanese Reactor Leak Seem to Fail
2011-04-03.abstract: A struggle to plug a gush of tainted water appeared to fall short as the radiation threat spread.

2011-04-03.title: Spain’s Prime Minister Will Not Seek Third Term
2011-04-03.abstract: Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero put an end to months of speculation about his political future amid an economic downturn.

2011-04-03.title: 2 Qaddafi Sons Are Said to Offer Plan to Push Father Out
2011-04-03.abstract: At least two sons of Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi have a resolution for a constitutional democracy under Seif al-Islam el-Qaddafi, a diplomat and a Libyan official said.

2011-04-03.title: Cricket Victory Brings Relief From Scandal in India
2011-04-03.abstract: Saturday night’s victory is providing a respite from what has become known as India’s “season of scams.”

2011-04-04.title: U.S. Shifts to Seek Removal of Yemen’s Leader, an Ally
2011-04-04.abstract: The United States, which long supported Yemen’s president, even in the face of recent protests, has shifted positions.

2011-04-04.title: In Spain, Stepping Down in Bid to Retain Power
2011-04-04.abstract: Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero is gambling that his decision not to seek a third term will bolster his party’s chances to retain power.

2011-04-04.title: As Fighting Continues The Injured Received Medical Treatment
2011-04-04.abstract: The ship, carrying hundreds from Misrata to Turkey for care, made a brief stop in Benghazi, where rebel youth gathered on the dock to welcome them and seek news from an embattled city that has been largely cut off from the world for weeks.

2011-04-04.title: New State Museum Sanitizes China’s Past
2011-04-04.abstract: Even with a $380 million overhaul, the reopened National Museum of China exemplifies the Communist Party’s determination to suppress alternative points of view and control the narrative of history.

2011-04-04.title: At China’s New Museum, History Toes Party Line
2011-04-04.abstract: The National Museum of China showcases the Communist Party’s efforts to control the narrative of history.

2011-04-05.title: Sanctions Are Dropped Against Libyan Defector
2011-04-05.abstract: The bank accounts of Moussa Koussa were unfrozen to encourage other defections, but concerns about ties to terrorism have raised questions about the move.

2011-04-05.title: U.N. Plane Crashes in Congo, Killing 32
2011-04-05.abstract: The passengers included peacekeepers, United Nations officials, humanitarian workers and electoral assistants.

2011-04-05.title: After Rape Report in Libya, Woman Sees Benefit in Publicity
2011-04-05.abstract: A woman who took her report of a rape by militiamen to the news media says the attention may have led officials to treat her better.

2011-04-05.title: Israel Indicts Gaza Man on Terror-Linked Counts
2011-04-05.abstract: Israel on Monday handed down an indictment against Dirar Abu Sisi, a Gaza engineer who is accused of developing rockets and missiles on behalf of Hamas.

2011-04-05.title: At Age 84, a City’s Last Geisha Defies Time and a 4th Tsunami
2011-04-05.abstract: Tsuyako Ito’s mother carried her in her first tsunami in 1933, but in March, it was an admirer who took the last geisha of Kamaishi, Japan, to higher ground.

2011-04-06.title: Syrian Rights Groups Raise Toll From Unrest
2011-04-06.abstract: Human rights groups raised their estimates of the death toll from unrest in Syria, as protest organizers there continued their call for more demonstrations against President Bashar al-Assad.

2011-04-06.title: White House Assails Pakistan Effort on Militants
2011-04-06.abstract: After years of work with the Pakistani military to defeat militants, victory over the thriving insurgency remains uncertain, the Obama administration said.

2011-04-06.title: When a Punch Line Is No Longer a Lifeline for Egyptians
2011-04-06.abstract: The sudden turn from humor points to a sense of revolution fatigue that has swept over a nation where people had hoped for overnight change.

2011-04-06.title: U.S. Sees Array of New Threats at Japan’s Nuclear Plant
2011-04-06.abstract: The nuclear plant in Japan faces an array of fresh threats that could persist indefinitely or increase as a result of stabilization efforts, according to the Nuclear Regulatory Commission.

2011-04-06.title: Through Political Prism, Iraqis Grieve
2011-04-06.abstract: The assault on the provincial council in Tikrit was a stark reminder of the country’s stubborn insurgency and an army and police force that still have glaring deficiencies and suspect loyalties.

2011-04-07.title: Bid for State of Palestine Gets Support From I.M.F.
2011-04-07.abstract: The International Monetary Fund says the Palestinian Authority is fully capable of running the economy of an independent state.

2011-04-07.title: As China Ages, Birthrate Policy May Prove Difficult to Reverse
2011-04-07.abstract: As China’s population grows older, the government may relax the one-child policy that has lowered the country’s birthrate, but that may not greatly change what is now established practice.

2011-04-07.title: Building a Stage for Mideast Peace Before the Final Curtain
2011-04-07.abstract: Juliano Mer Khamis was a renowned actor, director and political activist who embodied the Israeli-Arab conflict and embraced its complexities in a way that few could.

2011-04-07.title: Bahrain’s Rulers Tighten Their Grip on Battered Opposition
2011-04-07.abstract: The splintered Shiite opposition in Bahrain is seeking ways to regain momentum after the nation’s Sunni rulers crushed opposition protests last month.

2011-04-07.title: Sudan Says Israel Launched a Missile Strike on a Car, Killing 2
2011-04-07.abstract: Sudanese officials said a missile struck a car traveling from the Red Sea city of Port Sudan, obliterating the vehicle and killing two people who were inside.

2011-04-08.title: Tycoon’s Project: Nimby With a French Accent
2011-04-08.abstract: Bernard Arnault, who runs LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton, is in a battle with a neighborhood group that wants to stop his museum.

2011-04-08.title: Egypt's Re-Revolution
2011-04-08.abstract: Egyptians continue fighting to root out prominent allies of Hosni Mubarak, suggesting that it is more difficult to change a system than to oust a leader.

2011-04-08.title: International Court Hears Charges in Kenyan Election Violence
2011-04-08.abstract: Three prominent Kenyans appeared in The Hague to hear charges that they bore responsibility for the chaos and violence that followed Kenya’s disputed 2007 elections.

2011-04-08.title: Egypt Protests Go On, Seeking New Beginning
2011-04-08.abstract: In government ministries, factories and universities, protests have focused on those viewed as surrogates of Hosni Mubarak.

2011-04-08.title: Before Britain’s Royal Wedding, Rumors Fly but Lips Are Sealed
2011-04-08.abstract: In the final weeks before Prince William and Kate Middleton marry, wedding details are anyone’s guess.

2011-04-09.title: Tens of Thousands Protest in Yemen
2011-04-09.abstract: More than 100,000 people gathered for dueling demonstrations over the fate of President Ali Abdullah Saleh, who faces a rising tide of international pressure to step down.

2011-04-09.title: U.S. to Keep 3 Brigades in Europe
2011-04-09.abstract: President Obama has revised a Bush administration plan from 2004 that would have halved the number of Army combat units deployed in Europe.

2011-04-09.title: Georgia: Shootout in Abkhazia
2011-04-09.abstract: A Russian border guard and two Georgians were killed in a shootout in Abkhazia, a breakaway Georgian territory, Russia’s Federal Security Service said.

2011-04-09.title: Lack of Data Heightens Japan’s Nuclear Crisis
2011-04-09.abstract: Nearly a month after the nuclear accident, the danger posed by the Fukushima Daiichi plant is still unclear.

2011-04-09.title: Allies of Zimbabwe’s President Push for Quick Vote
2011-04-09.abstract: With no credible successor to unite the quarrelsome factions that threaten to splinter the country’s ruling party, Zimbabwe hurtles into another violent political season.

2011-04-10.title: Brazil Mourns Children Gunned Down at School
2011-04-10.abstract: Burial services were held for 11 of 12 children shot to death Thursday in a working-class neighborhood of Rio.

2011-04-10.title: China Detains Church Members Over Public Praying
2011-04-10.abstract: The police detained dozens of members of an underground Protestant church after the congregation tried to pray in a public plaza in Beijing.

2011-04-10.title: Rebels and NATO Strikes Repel Assault on Key Libyan Town
2011-04-10.abstract: Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi’s military forces appeared to falter in a second day of assault against Ajdabiya.

2011-04-10.title: Official Defends Japan’s Response to Disasters, Calling Them Unprecedented
2011-04-10.abstract: A top government official on Sunday said the scale of the earthquake and tsunami that killed thousands and damaged a nuclear power station far surpassed what experts had planned for.

2011-04-10.title: Malaysian Grand Prix Entertains as Vettel Wins Again
2011-04-10.abstract: Sebastian Vettel led from pole to victory for the second time this season, as the new rules of Formula One nevertheless provided more excitement – and confusion.

2011-04-11.title: City, Destroyed and Yet Hopeful, Begins to Move On
2011-04-11.abstract: Minamisoma is coming back to life as survivors begin to return, walking through the debris to look for belongings or say prayers for those who did not make it.

2011-04-11.abstract: China has quietly but surely established a foothold in Suriname, a tiny corner of South America, with emigration and economic aid.

2011-04-11.abstract: In Abidjan, areas held by forces loyal to Laurent Gbagbo, as well as the entrenched strongman’s residence, were struck by missiles fired from helicopters.

2011-04-11.title: Israel and Hamas Consider Cease-Fire
2011-04-11.abstract: As the two sides signaled that they were willing to restore calm, militants in Gaza fired about 10 rockets and mortar shells into southern Israel, but Israel did not immediately respond.

2011-04-11.title: Nationalist Candidate Appears to Be Leading in Peru’s Presidential Vote
2011-04-11.abstract: Ollanta Humala, a nationalist critical of the expanding influence of foreign companies in Peru, appears to be in the lead, with a June runoff seeming likely.

2011-04-12.title: Karzai Blames Western Firms for Kabul Bank’s Troubles
2011-04-12.abstract: President Hamid Karzai on Monday denounced Western accounting firms and advisers for corruption and failing to detect Kabul Bank’s troubles.

2011-04-12.title: Iraq Steps Back Onto the Regional Stage
2011-04-12.abstract: As Baghdad prepares for the Arab League summit, the influence of the United States is showing signs of waning.

2011-04-12.title: Britain: Sub Crewman Arraigned
2011-04-12.abstract: A 22-year-old navy submarine crewman appeared in magistrate’s court in Southampton on Monday, charged with killing one of the vessel’s senior officers and wounding three crewmen in a burst of automatic rifle fire.

2011-04-12.title: Possible Libya Stalemate Puts Stress on U.S. Policy
2011-04-12.abstract: President Obama’s first “humanitarian war” is eluding the quick resolution that was envisioned.

2011-04-12.title: Syrian University Protests Violently Suppressed
2011-04-12.abstract: Protests spread for the first time to a university campus and were forced back a day after the government said that it was using force against protesters.

2011-04-13.title: Diplomatic Breakdown Amid Bieber Fever in Israel
2011-04-13.abstract: A planned meeting between Justin Bieber and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel was called off.

2011-04-13.title: Syria Presses Crackdown in Two Cities on Coast
2011-04-13.abstract: Checkpoints blocked access to Baniyas, and forces carried out a violent raid on the village of Bayda.

2011-04-13.abstract: France and Britain openly called on the alliance and its partners to intensify airstrikes, prompting an unusual public retort from NATO’s command.

2011-04-13.abstract: Libyan rebel leaders rejected any suggestion of talks with Moussa Koussa, Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi’s former confidant who fled Libya for Britain.

2011-04-13.abstract: Problems remain with long-term regular cooling of the reactors and delivering water to the spent-fuel pools, the chairman of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission said.

2011-04-14.title: Strongman Out, Ivory Coast Is Reviving
2011-04-14.abstract: With the arrest of Laurent Gbagbo, some residents expressed hope and relief that the five-month crisis was now over.

2011-04-14.abstract: Syria’s protest movement is spreading, with demonstrations at a university and a march by women demanding the release of detainees.

2011-04-14.title: Restoring Sendai’s Airport

2011-04-14.abstract: Pentagon officials said that American warplanes had continued to strike targets in Libya even after the Obama administration said the United States was stepping back.

2011-04-14.abstract: Young lawmakers in Iraq are running up against an ossified political elite still dominated by exiles.

2011-04-15.title: Somalia, Wobbly on Ground, Seeks Control of Its Airspace
2011-04-15.abstract: The country hopes to collect millions of dollars in flyover fees if it regains authority over traffic in its airspace, which the United Nations has controlled since 1996.

2011-04-15.title: A Motley Consensus on the Afghanistan Line Item
2011-04-15.abstract: The war in Afghanistan has made for some unusual bedfellows, especially when it comes to how to pay for it.

2011-04-15.title: 16 Officers Arrested in Mexico Deaths
2011-04-15.abstract: The officers were charged with protecting a criminal gang suspected of murdering dozens of people and dumping their bodies in farmland about 90 miles south of the Texas border.

2011-04-15.title: Japan Opposition Leader Urges Premier to Resign
2011-04-15.abstract: The call for Prime Minister Naoto Kan’s resignation ended an uneasy political truce forged after the devastating earthquake and tsunami.

2011-04-15.title: Syrian Government Offers Mixed Message to Protesters
2011-04-15.abstract: Even as changes such as amnesty for some prisoners were announced, human rights activists said organizers of the protest movement were being detained.

2011-04-16.title: The World This Week
2011-04-16.abstract: The fall of Laurent Gbagbo; NATO shows signs of strain in its Libyan campaign; Qatar and the Arab Spring.

2011-04-16.title: Critic of War Effort Does His Homework, Up Close
2011-04-16.abstract: Bing West has strong words about the handling of the current Afghan war, but he’s no armchair general — he still dodges bullets with the troops.

2011-04-16.title: Somali Pirates Release Some Prisoners
2011-04-16.abstract: More than $3 million in ransom was received by the hijackers, who continue to hold some Indian hostages in hopes of winning the release of pirates held by India.

2011-04-16.abstract: Anti-riot police officers clashed with student protesters at Uganda’s most prestigious university Friday as students demonstrated against tuition increases and widespread government corruption.

2011-04-16.title: China: 54 Detained in Crackdown
2011-04-16.abstract: At least 54 people have been detained in the current crackdown on progressive thought and speech, according to a statement released by China Human Rights Defenders.

2011-04-17.title: Brazilian Criticizes Wealthy Nations’ Policies
2011-04-17.abstract: Finance Minister Guido Mantega said the United States and other countries were trying to “export their way” out of economic problems.

2011-04-17.title: Rebels of Misurata
2011-04-17.abstract: Libyans in Misurata have managed for almost two months to prevent their city from being overrun, and some of the reasons are visible on the street.

2011-04-17.title: Tokyo Utility Lays Out Plan for Its Reactors
2011-04-17.abstract: As Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton visited, Tokyo Electric said it hoped to stabilize the reactors.

2011-04-17.title: Lewis Hamilton Breaks Vettel’s Domination, Winning Chinese Grand Prix
2011-04-17.abstract: Lewis Hamilton drove to a fabulous race victory at the Chinese Grand Prix outside Shanghai on Sunday, taking the lead in the final laps from Sebastian Vettel.

2011-04-17.title: Counting Starts in Nigeria After Vote
2011-04-17.abstract: Results from a watershed election began to trickle in after the voting unfolded peacefully the day before.

2011-04-18.title: Illicit Church, Evicted, Tries to Buck Beijing
2011-04-18.abstract: A church that raised $4 million from its members to buy a house of worship fell under the disapproving scrutiny of the Communist Party.

2011-04-18.title: French Colonial Past Casts Long Shadow Over Policy in Africa
2011-04-18.abstract: Recent military interventions in Libya and Ivory Coast have prompted some to ask if France is returning to the old days of colonial politics, known as Françafrique.

2011-04-18.title: A Procession of Wartime Trauma
2011-04-18.abstract: In the rebel holdout city of Misurata, a triage tent acted as a way station for wounded rebel fighters and civilians alike.

2011-04-18.title: In a Medical Tent in Libya, a Grim Procession
2011-04-18.abstract: An overcrowded tent in the rebel holdout city of Misurata illustrates the grim results of modern weapons on human life.

2011-04-18.title: A City in Libya Takes Halting Steps to Democracy
2011-04-18.abstract: On a seaside boulevard in Benghazi, pictures of the missing and tents housing a range of groups testify to a new grass-roots populism, as well as the sacrifices made for it.

2011-04-19.title: Robert Mugabe Hounds Rivals in Zimbabwe, Parties Say
2011-04-19.abstract: President Robert Mugabe and his party are engaged in a campaign of intimidation and harassment intended to drive his opponents out of office.

2011-04-19.title: Lack of Success Terminates Study in Africa of AIDS Prevention in Women
2011-04-19.abstract: Early results of a trial of Truvada, a medication that had showed promising results among gay men, showed no evidence that it worked for women.

2011-04-19.title: In a Changing Cuba, Many Remain Skeptical
2011-04-19.abstract: As Raúl Castro issues blunt calls for reform, Cubans seem ambivalent that the end result will change much.

2011-04-19.title: Talks on U.S. Presence in Afghanistan After Pullout Unnerve Region
2011-04-19.abstract: Afghanistan’s neighbors, including Iran and Russia, fear an agreement will lead to permanent bases.

2011-04-19.title: In This Fairy Tale, Not One, but Two Queens in Waiting
2011-04-19.abstract: At Britain’s royal wedding of Prince William and Kate Middleton, one of the most uneasy seats in the church may be the one occupied by Camilla, Duchess of Cornwall.

2011-04-20.title: South Africa Exults Abroad but Frets at Home
2011-04-20.abstract: South Africa has joined an elite group of emerging powers, but at home, it faces an erosion of confidence in the government.

2011-04-20.title: On Journey, Young Tunisians Need Only a Final Destination
2011-04-20.abstract: In a French-Italian political soap opera, economic migrants are seeking opportunity in a place that does not want them.

2011-04-20.title: U.S. Engineers Cite Lengthy Cleanup in Japan
2011-04-20.abstract: Veterans of the Three Mile Island cleanup said that a much larger task faced the Japanese engineers who are trying contain and secure the damaged Fukushima Daiichi reactors.

2011-04-20.title: Belarus: Currency to Be Traded
2011-04-20.abstract: Belarus announced Tuesday that it would allow its flagging currency to trade freely between banks, all but ensuring a devaluation of the ruble in an effort to assuage an increasingly dire economic situation.

2011-04-20.title: Chile: Evidence on Priest Seized
2011-04-20.abstract: The police in Santiago have raided the home and offices of a lawyer representing a Roman Catholic priest found guilty by the Vatican of sexually molesting under-age boys.

2011-04-21.title: Mubarak Leaves an Air of Wistfulness
2011-04-21.abstract: There is an undercurrent of discomfort with how Hosni Mubarak has been treated since being forced from power in Egypt.

2011-04-21.title: European Union’s Budget Proposal Stokes Anger
2011-04-21.abstract: A proposal seeking 4.9 percent more money in a time of widespread belt-tightening stirred up some of the bloc’s member states.

2011-04-21.title: In Spain, Women Find a Place in the Procession
2011-04-21.abstract: Seville’s processions of hooded penitents have put the Andalusian capital at the center stage of Catholic celebrations. And this year, the oldest of its brotherhoods is allowing women to join in the march for the first time.

2011-04-21.title: Rebels Attack Loyalists in Misurata
2011-04-21.abstract: Rebel forces assaulted the downtown positions of troops loyal to Muammar el-Qaddafi in Misurata, Libya.

2011-04-21.title: China’s Plan to Cut Taxes Puts Burden on Wealthy
2011-04-21.abstract: The proposal, which could become law this year, would raise the minimum income required to pay the tax to $450 a month from $315.

2011-04-22.title: Briefly Returning to the Nuclear Zone
2011-04-22.abstract: Residents who lived near the damaged Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant flocked to the area on Thursday ahead of a midnight evacuation deadline imposed by the government.

2011-04-22.title: Finland’s Turn to Right Sends Shivers Through Euro Zone
2011-04-22.abstract: The obscure True Finns party grabbed a share of the government in elections last week after campaigning on the evils of the European Union and its bailouts of Greece and Ireland.

2011-04-22.abstract: Prime Minister Julia Gillard’s government appears intent on giving the armed forces their biggest shake-up in more than a decade, including making all combat roles available to women.

2011-04-22.title: Witness to Bishop’s Death Freed in Argentina
2011-04-22.abstract: The Argentine president’s order to find Víctor Martínez is believed to have scared off the kidnappers.

2011-04-22.title: Securing a Former Taliban Stronghold
2011-04-22.abstract: American soldiers take tactics that helped secure a once-bombarded military base to nearby villages in an effort to push out insurgents.

2011-04-23.title: Terrorism Suspect Is Captured in a Raid in Afghanistan
2011-04-23.abstract: Military officials called the man a central conduit between the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan and senior Taliban leaders in Afghanistan and Pakistan.

2011-04-23.title: Protests Across Syria
2011-04-23.abstract: Large protests began in March in the town of Dara’a, where citizens were outraged by the arrest of students for writing graffiti.

2011-04-23.title: Ex-Blackwater Guards Face Renewed Charges
2011-04-23.abstract: The manslaughter charges against four military contractors, stemming from a 2007 shooting in Baghdad, will be returned to a lower court for review.

2011-04-23.title: In Media’s Wedding Frenzy, Hints of Viewer Fatigue
2011-04-23.abstract: As the royal wedding day draws near, there are signs that the media frenzy may outpace interest in the United States, and some disdain is appearing among the British as well.

2011-04-23.title: Royal Wedding Wine May Be Bubbly and English
2011-04-23.abstract: The once-absurd notion of fine English wine is becoming a more common one, with some wondering whether sparkling wine will replace Champagne at the royal wedding.

2011-04-24.title: Haitians Forced Out of Tents to Homes Just as Precarious
2011-04-24.abstract: More than half of the Haitians driven into camps by the 2010 earthquake have moved out, but most of them appear to have been forced out or to have left to escape crime and living conditions.

2011-04-24.title: Palestinian Police Kill Israeli Visiting West Bank Holy Site
2011-04-24.abstract: The Palestinian police shot and killed one Israeli and wounded four others early Sunday after the Israelis surreptitiously visited a Jewish holy site inside a Palestinian-controlled area.

2011-04-24.title: Thailand and Cambodia Clash Again in Border Dispute
2011-04-24.abstract: The United Nations called on both countries to settle the conflict peacefully as both sides accused each other of making the first move in what was mostly a long-range artillery duel.

2011-04-24.title: China Detains Church Members at Easter Services
2011-04-24.abstract: The authorities stepped up a three-week campaign against an underground Christian church by holding hundreds in their homes and taking 36 others into custody.

2011-04-24.title: More Syrians Are Missing, Hinting at a Wider Crackdown
2011-04-24.abstract: Dozens have vanished since Friday, activists said, another indication that the government’s decision to lift emergency rule may prove mostly rhetoric.

2011-04-25.title: Berber Rebels in Libya’s West Face Long Odds Against Qaddafi
2011-04-25.abstract: Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi has long harbored antagonism toward a group of mostly Ibadi Muslims in a country that is majority Sunni.

2011-04-25.title: Libyan Rebels Say They Have Control of Misurata
2011-04-25.abstract: The rebels say they drove government forces out of the western city, though shelling has continued, while the government says that its forces withdrew while a cease-fire is being negotiated.

2011-04-25.abstract: A group of truck drivers who helped stage a three-day strike last week over rising oil prices and high government fees said the city’s promise to cut some freight transportation fees was not enough.

2011-04-25.title: Classified Files Offer New Insights Into Detainees
2011-04-25.abstract: Classified assessments of detainees at Guantánamo Bay prison obtained by The Times give the fullest public picture to date of the prisoners held there.

2011-04-25.title: A Note to Readers: The Background
2011-04-25.abstract: The articles published today are based on more than 700 classified files on past and present detainees at the Guantánamo Bay prison.

2011-04-26.title: Poll Finds Egyptians Full of Hope About the Future
2011-04-26.abstract: Egyptians are looking forward to this fall’s elections, but remain divided over the role of Islam in their public life.

2011-04-26.title: Guest List Prompts Talk of Royal Snubs
2011-04-26.abstract: A lack of space was the reported reason for excluding Britain’s two last prime ministers, Tony Blair and Gordon Brown, from Prince William’s wedding.

2011-04-26.title: U.S. Faces a Challenge in Trying to Punish Syria
2011-04-26.abstract: There seems little left in the way of sanctions for Syria, and the Obama administration has so far refrained from seeking the departure of its president.

2011-04-26.title: Mauritania: Police Break Up Antigovernment Protest
2011-04-26.abstract: Using tear gas and batons, the police broke up an antigovernment demonstration of several hundred youths in the capital, Nouakchott, on Monday.

2011-04-26.title: Germany: Protesters Demand Stop to Nuclear Power
2011-04-26.abstract: An estimated 120,000 people demonstrated across Germany on Monday, protest organizers said, demanding an end to the use of nuclear power.

2011-04-27.title: Text Messages Proliferate as Threats in Iraq
2011-04-27.abstract: Cellphones are a common tool of intimidation, blackmail and sectarian warfare throughout Iraq, and the culprits are difficult to catch.

2011-04-27.title: British Law Used to Shush Scandal Has Become One
2011-04-27.abstract: Once a super injunction is served on a case, news outlets may not write about it.

2011-04-27.title: From a Qaddafi Daughter, a Glimpse Inside the Bunker
2011-04-27.abstract: In a rare interview, Aisha el-Qaddafi dismissed all talk of giving up power but provided insight into the mood of her increasingly isolated family.

2011-04-27.title: TimesCast | Qaddafi's Daughter Speaks
2011-04-27.abstract: In a rare interview, Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi's daughter, Aisha, gave insight into her family's struggle to hold on to power.

2011-04-27.title: Syria Tries to Defend Its Record to United Nations
2011-04-27.abstract: The nation’s envoy repeated the government’s claim that the unrest was the work of as yet unidentified foreign agitators trying to undermine Syria’s stability.

2011-04-28.title: Besieged in Misurata
2011-04-28.abstract: Migrant workers found passage out of Misurata, Libya, aboard a chartered ship as shelling and fighting continued.

2011-04-28.title: Panetta Comes Armed With Background in Budget Fights
2011-04-28.abstract: For Leon E. Panetta, the new leader of the Defense Department, the war at home will be with Congress over the Pentagon budget.

2011-04-28.title: Besieged in Misurata
2011-04-28.abstract: Migrant workers found passage out of Misurata, Libya, aboard a chartered ship as shelling and fighting continued.

2011-04-28.title: A Syrian Beacon Pays Price for Its Dissent
2011-04-28.abstract: Dara’a has become center stage of a Syrian uprising that has posed the greatest challenge to the Assad family’s four decades of rule.

2011-04-28.title: Protests and Strikes Grow in Yemen as Deal on Ouster Advances
2011-04-28.abstract: The turmoil mounted even as opposition and government officials were moving toward an agreement to usher President Ali Abdullah Saleh out of office in a month.

2011-04-29.title: To Lead Afghan War, Obama Chooses Marine Known for Swaying Sunnis in Iraq
2011-04-29.abstract: Lt. Gen. John R. Allen was President Obama’s first choice to succeed Gen. David H. Petraeus, in no small part for his work enticing Sunni tribal elders to join the American cause.

2011-04-29.title: Move to C.I.A. Puts Petraeus in Conflict With Pakistan
2011-04-29.abstract: Pakistani and American officials said Gen. David H. Petraeus’s selection as C.I.A. director could further inflame relations between the two nations.

2011-04-29.title: Trying to Stir Up a Popular Protest in China, From a Bedroom in Manhattan
2011-04-29.abstract: A 27-year-old cyberwarrior in Upper Manhattan is still pushing for reform in China despite the crackdown there.

2011-04-29.title: 2 Russians Guilty of Killing Rights Lawyer and Journalist
2011-04-29.abstract: A jury found that the ideologically driven defendants considered the victims enemies of Russia.

2011-04-29.title: In Shift, Egypt Warms to Iran and Hamas, Israel’s Foes
2011-04-29.abstract: Egypt is planning to open the blockaded border with Gaza and normalizing relations with two of Israel and the West’s Islamist foes, Hamas and Iran.

2011-04-30.title: Former Agent for Pinochet Is Found Slain in Argentina
2011-04-30.abstract: Enrique Arancibia Clavel, who was convicted in 1974 of organizing the assassination of a leading critic of Gen. Augusto Pinochet, was found in his apartment with multiple stab wounds.

2011-04-30.title: China: Fears of Crackdown on Music Events
2011-04-30.abstract: The cancellation of a music festival this weekend in Suzhou has some musicians and festival organizers fearing they may be swept up in the government crackdown on dissent.

2011-04-30.title: Russia: 10 Suspects Killed in Caucasus Raid
2011-04-30.abstract: Russian security forces said Friday that at least 10 Islamist militants had been killed in a special operation in the volatile and mostly Muslim North Caucasus region.

2011-04-30.title: The World This Week
2011-04-30.abstract: A discussion of Friday's royal wedding.

2011-04-30.title: Japan’s Prime Minister Defends Handling of Crisis
2011-04-30.abstract: Naoto Kan’s remarks came after an adviser resigned to protest what he called unsafe measures.
